title: Exergen TAT-5000S Series Thermometer - Low Battery / Blank Display Condition
description: Troubleshooting a bAtt indication or blank display caused by a depleted battery, battery connection problem, or damaged thermometer.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the thermometer from clinical use until proper display operation and temperature measurement are confirmed. Provide staff with a known-working thermometer for patient temperature assessment. Do not troubleshoot or verify operation during active patient care. Expected outcome: Patient temperature measurements continue using reliable alternate equipment.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Display Condition
steps.instructions: Press and hold the scan button while observing the display. Identify whether the thermometer shows: bAtt, indicating a low battery condition; a completely blank display, which Exergen identifies as no battery or a very low battery condition; or Err, which indicates a different processing or operational issue. Expected outcome: The reported symptom is accurately identified before corrective action is taken. Exergen identifies bAtt as low battery and a blank display as no or very low battery in the TAT-5000S display diagnostics chart.

steps.title: Inspect the Thermometer Externally
steps.instructions: Examine the thermometer for: cracked or damaged housing; signs of a drop or impact; fluid intrusion or dried contamination near the battery compartment; an unreadable or damaged display window; and evidence of unusual heat, odor, or battery leakage. Do not continue troubleshooting if the unit appears physically damaged, has been immersed, or shows battery leakage affecting the device. Expected outcome: No external damage is found that would make further functional checking inappropriate.

steps.title: Access and Inspect the Battery Compartment
steps.instructions: With the thermometer removed from patient use, insert the end of a bent paper clip into the side pinhole to release the battery compartment door. Remove the battery and inspect for: loose battery connection; corroded or contaminated battery contacts; swollen, leaking, or damaged battery; and damaged battery compartment door or retention features. Dispose of a depleted or leaking battery according to hospital policy. Expected outcome: The battery compartment is clean, intact, and suitable for a replacement battery.

steps.title: Replace the Battery
steps.instructions: Install a new, high-quality alkaline 9-volt battery in the same location and orientation as the removed battery. Reinstall the battery compartment cover securely. Exergen specifies the battery as the only user-serviceable component and instructs use of a high-quality alkaline 9-volt replacement battery. Expected outcome: The battery is securely connected and the compartment cover is fully seated.

steps.title: Verify Display Startup and Normal Scan Response
steps.instructions: Press the scan button after battery replacement. Confirm that: the display activates; the bAtt indication is no longer present; the thermometer enters normal scanning operation when the button is held; and the display remains legible and does not intermittently blank out. If the display returns to normal and remains stable, continue to the functional check and stop replacing parts. Expected outcome: The thermometer powers normally with a clear, stable display.

steps.title: Perform a Functional Check Away from Patient Care
steps.instructions: With the unit clean and removed from active patient use, perform a basic operational scan according to department procedure or use a temperature verification method available to Clinical Engineering. Confirm that: the device responds consistently to scan-button activation; a temperature value is displayed after scanning; no bAtt, blank display, or Err condition reappears; and the reading is reasonable or passes the available verification check. Do not return the thermometer to use if performance is intermittent or the reading cannot be trusted. Expected outcome: The thermometer displays stable operation and is suitable for return to service according to department policy.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Exergen TAT-5000S displayed bAtt and then became blank when the scan button was pressed.
CCR.Cause: Found depleted 9-volt battery with no visible thermometer damage or battery-compartment corrosion.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced the battery with a new alkaline 9-volt battery, verified normal display and scan response, and returned the thermometer to service.

helpfulDetails: Display condition observed: bAtt, blank, intermittent, or Err
Scan button response before and after battery replacement
Battery type and condition removed
New alkaline 9-volt battery installed
Battery compartment inspected for corrosion or leakage
Housing inspected for drop or fluid damage
Functional check result
Final device status: returned to service or removed from service

title: Exergen TAT-5000S Series Thermometer - ERR PROCESSING ERROR DURING STARTUP OR READING
description: Troubleshooting an Err processing error appearing during startup or temperature measurement before the thermometer is returned for repair.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the thermometer from clinical use while the Err message is present. Do not use the affected TAT-5000S for patient assessment until normal operation has been confirmed. Provide clinical staff with another known-working thermometer. Expected outcome: Temperature assessment continues using reliable equipment while the affected unit is evaluated. Why it matters: Exergen identifies Err as a processing error, and a thermometer displaying this condition cannot be trusted for clinical readings until the error clears.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Displayed Condition
steps.instructions: Activate the thermometer and confirm that the display shows Err, rather than: bAtt for low battery; Blank display for no or very low battery; HI, LO, HI A, or LO A for temperature or ambient range conditions. Record whether Err occurs immediately at startup, only when the scan button is pressed, or intermittently during repeated attempts. Expected outcome: The reported issue is confirmed as a processing error and not a different display diagnostic condition.

steps.title: Restart the Thermometer
steps.instructions: Allow the display to clear, then activate the thermometer again and attempt a test scan away from patient care. If Err does not return and the thermometer completes a normal scan display sequence, proceed to functional verification before returning it to use. If Err returns, continue troubleshooting. Expected outcome: A temporary processing interruption may clear after restart. Why it matters: The manufacturer’s display diagnostics instruction for Err begins with restarting the thermometer.

steps.title: Inspect the Thermometer for External Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Examine the device for: Evidence of being dropped or struck; Cracked housing or damaged probe head; Fluid intrusion or signs the device was submerged; Corrosion or contamination in the battery compartment; Heavy residue, moisture, or damage on the sensor lens. Do not use the thermometer if damage, fluid exposure, or electrical shock exposure is suspected. Expected outcome: No visible external condition is present that would make continued evaluation unsafe or unreliable. Why it matters: Exergen advises against use when the thermometer is damaged, exposed to temperature extremes, electrically shocked, immersed, or not working properly.

steps.title: Verify Battery Condition and Connection
steps.instructions: Open the battery compartment according to the device design and inspect the installed 9V battery and connector. If the battery is weak, aged, loose, corroded, or of unknown condition, replace it with a new high-quality alkaline 9V battery. Reinstall the battery cover, then restart the thermometer. If the unit starts normally and no longer displays Err, continue to functional verification. Expected outcome: The thermometer powers up consistently without displaying Err. Why it matters: Battery replacement is the only user-serviceable component identified by Exergen for the TAT-5000S series.

steps.title: Check the Operating Environment
steps.instructions: Confirm the thermometer has not been exposed to unusual temperature extremes or used in an inappropriate environment. Move the device to a typical indoor clinical environment and allow it to stabilize before restarting it. The TAT-5000S operating environment is specified as 16 to 40°C / 61 to 104°F. Also move the thermometer away from obvious sources of electromagnetic interference, including nearby high-frequency surgical equipment or MRI environments. Expected outcome: The thermometer is evaluated under appropriate environmental conditions without nearby interference concerns. Why it matters: Temperature extremes and electromagnetic disturbances can interfere with proper operation, and Exergen specifies environmental limitations for the device.

steps.title: Clean the Sensor Lens if Contaminated
steps.instructions: If the sensor lens is visibly dirty, greasy, or moist, clean it gently with a cotton swab dipped in alcohol, following facility cleaning practices and manufacturer guidance. Do not use bleach or other cleaning solutions on the sensor lens, and do not immerse the thermometer. Allow the lens to dry, then restart the thermometer and attempt a test scan. Expected outcome: The sensor lens is clean and dry, and the unit completes a normal test scan without Err. Why it matters: Lens contamination primarily affects reading accuracy, but it should be ruled out during external evaluation of a thermometer failing during measurement.

steps.title: Perform a Safe Functional Check
steps.instructions: With the thermometer off the patient-care workflow, activate the unit and confirm: The display initializes normally; The scanning indication appears during a test scan; A temperature result is displayed without Err; The error does not return during several repeat activations. If available within department process, perform calibration verification using the appropriate Exergen calibration verification equipment before return to clinical use, especially if there is concern about accuracy after the event. Expected outcome: The thermometer operates consistently without recurrence of the processing error.

steps.title: Stop if the Err Message Returns or Persists
steps.instructions: If Err remains after restart, battery replacement, environmental evaluation, cleaning, and basic functional checks, stop troubleshooting at the external level. Do not open the device or attempt internal repair, adjustment, or recalibration. Expected outcome: The device is prevented from returning to patient care with an unresolved processing fault. Why it matters: Exergen states that persistent Err requires return for repair, and that internal service or adjustment is not to be performed by the user or facility technician beyond battery replacement.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Exergen TAT-5000S displays Err during startup and will not provide a usable temperature reading.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed persistent Err processing error after restart, known-good 9V battery replacement, external inspection, lens cleaning, and evaluation in a normal clinical environment.
CCR.Resolution: Removed thermometer from service, labeled unit Out of Service, and routed it for Exergen repair due to persistent processing error.

helpfulDetails: Exact display message: Err
Whether error occurred at startup or during scanning
Battery condition and whether a new alkaline 9V battery was installed
Visible damage, drop history, fluid exposure, or corrosion
Sensor lens condition and cleaning performed
Environmental or electromagnetic interference concerns
Whether normal scans were obtained after restart
Whether the error recurred during repeated checks
Final device status: returned to use or removed from service

title: Exergen TAT-5000S Series Thermometer - HI, LO, HI A, or LO A Temperature/Ambient Range Messages
description: Troubleshooting target-temperature or ambient-temperature range messages caused by environment, measurement conditions, sensor contamination, or device malfunction.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the message occurred during patient care, have clinical staff obtain a temperature using another approved thermometer according to facility procedure. Do not troubleshoot, repeat-test, or validate the affected thermometer on an active patient. Expected outcome: Patient temperature assessment continues using a reliable alternate device while the Exergen thermometer is evaluated.

steps.title: Identify the Exact Display Message
steps.instructions: With the thermometer removed from patient use, reproduce or confirm the displayed message and document it exactly: HI — Measured target temperature is above 43 °C / 110 °F. LO — Measured target temperature is below 16 °C / 61 °F. HI A — Ambient temperature is above 40 °C / 104 °F. LO A — Ambient temperature is below 16 °C / 61 °F. These are documented display diagnostic indications for the TAT-5000S-US Series. Expected outcome: Clinical Engineering distinguishes a target-reading range message from an ambient-environment range message before continuing.

steps.title: Check the Operating Environment for HI A or LO A Messages
steps.instructions: For HI A or LO A, verify that the thermometer is being used in an environment between 16 and 40 °C / 61 and 104 °F. Inspect for recent exposure to: Cold storage areas or transport conditions; Hot vehicles, warming equipment, or direct sunlight; Temperature extremes during delivery or room relocation. Move the thermometer to a normal clinical room environment and allow it to acclimate before retesting. Expected outcome: Once the thermometer reaches an acceptable operating environment, the ambient-range message clears and normal scanning resumes. If the message clears, document the environmental cause and return the unit to service only after normal function is confirmed. Stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect the Thermometer for External Damage or Improper Exposure
steps.instructions: Examine the thermometer for: Cracked housing; Damaged probe cone; Moisture intrusion; Evidence of dropping or impact; Chemical residue or signs of improper cleaning. Exergen states that the thermometer should not be used if it has been damaged, exposed to temperature extremes, subjected to shock, or immersed in water. Expected outcome: The thermometer is physically intact and safe to continue evaluating. If damage, liquid intrusion, or shock exposure is found, remove the device from service and send it for repair evaluation. Stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check and Clean the Sensor Lens
steps.instructions: Inspect the probe lens for dirt, moisture, dried disinfectant residue, fingerprints, or film. Clean the sensor lens using a cotton swab lightly moistened with alcohol, applying light pressure only. Do not use bleach or immerse the thermometer. A contaminated lens can interfere with infrared heat detection and affect measurement accuracy. Expected outcome: The sensor lens is clean, dry, and unobstructed before retesting.

steps.title: Check Battery and Display Behavior
steps.instructions: Although HI, LO, HI A, and LO A are range indications rather than battery messages, confirm that: The display activates normally; There is no bAtt message; The display is not blank or intermittent; The 9V alkaline battery is secure and serviceable. Replace the battery if a battery-related symptom is also present. Expected outcome: Power instability is ruled out as a contributing condition.

steps.title: Evaluate HI or LO Messages Reported During Clinical Use
steps.instructions: For HI or LO, determine whether the message occurred during a patient measurement or while the thermometer was aimed at an inappropriate surface or environmental object. Ask for relevant details without retesting on a patient: Was the forehead covered by a hat, blanket, or hair immediately beforehand? Was there an ice pack, wet compress, or significant diaphoresis? Was the scan performed across exposed forehead skin? Was an alternate thermometer used to confirm the patient’s temperature? A HI or LO message can indicate a true temperature outside the displayed range or a measurement condition affecting the scan; it does not automatically confirm internal failure. The manufacturer advises confirming abnormal readings using repeat measurement or another TemporalScanner, while clinical care should use an alternate approved device when reliability is questioned. Expected outcome: The reported message is associated with either a plausible external measurement condition or an unexplained device behavior requiring further evaluation.

steps.title: Retest the Thermometer Under Controlled Conditions
steps.instructions: After confirming a normal room environment, clean lens, intact housing, and stable display, activate the thermometer away from active patient care and verify that it enters normal scanning operation. Normal scanning is indicated by the scanning display rather than a persistent HI, LO, HI A, or LO A message. If your department has an approved Exergen calibration verification method or kit, perform verification according to established procedure. Expected outcome: The thermometer operates normally without persistent range messages under appropriate environmental conditions. If normal operation is restored and verification passes, return the thermometer to service and document the corrective action. Stop troubleshooting.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported the Exergen TAT-5000S displayed LO A and would not provide a patient temperature reading in the treatment area.
CCR.Cause: Thermometer had been stored in a cold transport location and displayed a low ambient-temperature message until acclimated to the clinical room environment.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from patient use, allowed it to acclimate within the specified operating range, cleaned the probe lens, verified normal scanning operation, and returned the thermometer to service.

helpfulDetails: Exact message displayed: HI, LO, HI A, or LO A
Room temperature at time of failure
Recent transport or storage in a hot or cold area
Lens condition and cleaning performed
Battery condition or presence of bAtt message
Physical damage, moisture, or evidence of dropping
Whether an alternate thermometer confirmed the patient reading
Result of controlled functional or calibration verification
Final device status: returned to service or removed for repair

title: Exergen TAT-5000S Series Thermometer - Inaccurate or Inconsistent Temperature Readings
description: Troubleshooting unexpected high, low, or inconsistent temporal artery readings caused by lens contamination, technique, environment, battery condition, or failed accuracy verification.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the TAT-5000S from clinical use if staff report questionable readings or readings inconsistent with the patient’s condition. Provide staff with another known-working thermometer for clinical decision-making. Do not attempt to confirm questionable device performance on a patient who requires reliable temperature assessment. Expected outcome: Patient care continues using a trusted temperature-measurement device while the reported thermometer is evaluated.

steps.title: Verify the Complaint and Identify the Reading Pattern
steps.instructions: Ask clinical staff whether the thermometer is reading: Consistently low; Consistently high; Variable between repeated measurements; Different from another thermometer or measurement method. Check whether the comparison thermometer is the same type and calibration basis. A TAT-5000S with a serial number beginning with “A” is arterial calibrated; a unit marked Oral with a serial number beginning with “O” is programmed to display oral-equivalent readings. Comparing different calibration types can appear to show an accuracy problem when the units are not expected to display the same value. Expected outcome: The reported symptom is clearly defined, and a calibration-type mismatch is ruled out before further troubleshooting. If the issue is explained by comparing unlike calibration types, document the finding and stop.

steps.title: Inspect the Thermometer for External Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the probe cone, sensor lens, housing, display, battery compartment, and any attached communication cable on connected versions. Look for: Dirt, grease, moisture, or film on the lens; Cracked probe cone or damaged lens area; Evidence of a drop or impact; Fluid intrusion; Damaged housing or loose battery door. The TAT-5000S is a precision optical instrument, and lens contamination can directly affect infrared measurement accuracy. Exergen identifies a dirty lens as a common cause of low readings. Expected outcome: The unit has no obvious physical damage, and the lens condition is known. If the device is damaged, wet internally, or shows evidence of impact-related failure, remove it from service and send it for repair.

steps.title: Clean the Sensor Lens Correctly
steps.instructions: Using a cotton-tipped applicator lightly dampened with alcohol, gently clean the center of the sensor lens at the tip of the probe. Do not: Scrub forcefully; Use bleach or other cleaning solutions on the sensor lens; Immerse the thermometer in liquid; Autoclave the device. Allow the lens to dry, then reassess the reported condition. Exergen states that dirt, greasy film, or moisture on the lens can interfere with infrared transmission and affect accuracy. Expected outcome: The optical surface is clean and dry, eliminating the most common correctable cause of low or inconsistent readings. If cleaning resolves the reported reading problem, return the unit to service according to facility policy, document the correction, and stop.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition and Display Indicators
steps.instructions: Turn the thermometer on and observe the display. Check for: bAtt indication; Blank or intermittent display; Err message; Normal scanning indication during operation. Replace a low or questionable battery with a new, high-quality alkaline 9-volt battery. A low battery is identified by bAtt; a blank display may indicate no or very low battery. A persistent Err message requires repair evaluation. Expected outcome: The thermometer powers normally without battery or processing error indications. If battery replacement resolves the issue, perform a final functional verification, document the repair, and stop.

steps.title: Confirm Environmental Conditions Before Testing
steps.instructions: Confirm the thermometer has not recently been exposed to extreme cold, heat, or moisture. Allow the device to stabilize at room temperature before evaluating accuracy. The TAT-5000S operating environmental temperature range is 16 to 40°C / 61 to 104°F. For calibration verification, Exergen instructs that the reference instrument and thermometer being tested be allowed to acclimate to the same ambient temperature for at least 10 minutes, with additional time after exposure to very warm or cold conditions. Expected outcome: Accuracy evaluation is performed after environmental effects have been ruled out. If the complaint occurred only after improper storage or temperature exposure and readings normalize after stabilization, document the condition and stop.

steps.title: Rule Out Technique-Related Inconsistency
steps.instructions: With the device removed from clinical use, review the reported measurement technique with staff or evaluate technique using a controlled, non-clinical demonstration. Confirm that the operator: Places the probe flush at the center of the forehead; Holds the button while scanning straight across the forehead toward the hairline; Avoids scanning down the side of the face; Measures exposed skin without hair, hats, or blankets covering the site; Avoids rapidly repeating measurements on the same site without allowing recovery time; Accounts for diaphoresis or recent ice packs/wet compresses. Exergen identifies improper scan path, covered measurement sites, diaphoresis, recent cooling of the forehead, and rapid repeated measurements as possible causes of abnormal readings. Expected outcome: User technique and measurement-site conditions are not falsely creating inconsistent readings. If retraining resolves the reported issue and the thermometer performs normally, document the finding and stop.

steps.title: Check Measurement Units and Configuration
steps.instructions: Confirm that the display is in the expected unit of measure, either °F or °C, and verify that clinical staff were not interpreting a unit-conversion issue as an inaccurate reading. Also confirm whether the unit is arterial calibrated or oral-equivalent calibrated before comparing it with another thermometer or clinical protocol. Expected outcome: The thermometer’s displayed measurement type and calibration basis match the intended clinical workflow. If a configuration or interpretation issue is found, correct the workflow concern, document it, and stop.

steps.title: Perform Accuracy Verification if the Issue Remains
steps.instructions: If the lens is clean, the battery is good, technique and environmental concerns have been ruled out, and the readings remain questionable, perform accuracy verification using the appropriate Exergen Calibration Verification Kit when available. Before verification: Confirm the Certified Master and test thermometer have matching calibration types: A-to-A or O-to-O; Allow the verifier to stabilize; Acclimate both instruments to the same ambient temperature; Ensure both lenses are clean; Do not use disposable covers or sheaths during verification. Exergen states that TAT-5000S recalibration is normally not required, but calibration verification may be performed in Biomedical Engineering using its Calibration Verification Kit. Expected outcome: The thermometer either passes verification or is confirmed to require repair evaluation. If the unit passes verification, document the successful test and evaluate whether the original complaint resulted from technique, clinical comparison method, or environmental conditions.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Exergen TAT-5000S produced repeated temperature readings lower than expected compared with another temporal artery thermometer.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found visible residue on the probe sensor lens, which can interfere with infrared measurement and cause low readings.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the thermometer from use, cleaned the sensor lens with an alcohol-dampened cotton-tipped applicator, allowed the lens to dry, and confirmed consistent readings during follow-up verification.

helpfulDetails: Whether readings were high, low, or inconsistent
Comparison device type and calibration basis
Arterial or oral-equivalent calibration identification
Lens condition before and after cleaning
Battery status or bAtt indication
Presence of Err or other display messages
Room temperature and recent storage conditions
Reported operator technique concerns
Calibration verification result, if performed
Final device status: returned to service or removed for repair

title: Exergen TAT-5000S Series Thermometer - Damaged Probe, Housing, or RS-232/QR Interface Cable on Connected Versions
description: Evaluating visible physical damage or damaged connected-version cable components that may affect safe handling, temperature measurement, or data transmission.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the thermometer from clinical use before inspecting any reported crack, broken probe area, damaged lens, exposed cable conductors, or loose connector. Provide clinical staff with a known-working thermometer for patient temperature measurement. Expected outcome: Patient care continues using a device without suspected physical or communication-related damage. Why it matters: Exergen states that physical damage may require accuracy verification or return for service; a damaged device should not be trusted for clinical readings until evaluated.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the thermometer externally for: Cracks, separation, or sharp edges in the housing; Damage to the probe head or sensing lens; Loose, missing, or damaged battery compartment cover; Fluid intrusion evidence, discoloration, or residue; Loose or unstable cable connection on connected RS-232/QR versions; Cuts, crushed areas, exposed conductors, bent connector pins, or damaged strain relief on the interface cable. Do not open the instrument beyond routine access permitted for the battery compartment or externally replaceable connected-version cable. Expected outcome: The physical condition and exact location of damage are clearly identified and documented.

steps.title: Evaluate Probe Head and Sensing Area Condition
steps.instructions: Examine the probe head and lens carefully under good lighting. If the probe head, sensing lens, or surrounding housing is cracked, chipped, separated, scratched in the sensing area, or contaminated in a way that cannot be safely cleaned, stop troubleshooting. Expected outcome: A thermometer with an intact, clean sensing area may proceed to additional checks; a damaged sensing area is removed from service for evaluation. Why it matters: The TAT-5000S uses infrared sensing through the probe area, and physical damage may compromise confidence in temperature accuracy.

steps.title: Check for Safe Cleaning and Surface Integrity
steps.instructions: If no structural damage is found, confirm that the reported issue is not dried residue, adhesive, or removable contamination on the exterior surface or cable. Clean only according to approved facility and manufacturer-compatible cleaning practices. Do not submerge the thermometer in cleaning solution. Expected outcome: Surface contamination is removed without revealing cracks, softening, separation, or other true physical damage. Why it matters: Exergen specifically states that the thermometer must not be submerged in cleaning solution.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Cover and Basic Operation
steps.instructions: Confirm the battery cover closes securely and that the thermometer powers on normally. Observe the display during startup and a test activation for: Normal display response; bAtt, blank display, or Err; Intermittent operation when the instrument is handled normally. If the display is blank, shows persistent Err, or operation is intermittent after obvious external issues are ruled out, stop and escalate. Expected outcome: The unit powers on normally without error indications or intermittent behavior. Why it matters: The manufacturer’s diagnostic chart identifies persistent Err as requiring return for repair.

steps.title: Inspect the RS-232/QR Interface Cable on Connected Versions
steps.instructions: For TAT-5000S connected versions equipped with an RS-232/QR interface cable, inspect the full accessible cable length and both connection points. Look for: Torn or split insulation; Exposed conductors; Damaged connector housing; Broken retention features; Loose connection at the thermometer; Cable damage near strain-relief points; Intermittent data transmission when the cable is in its normal resting position. Do not tug, sharply bend, or manipulate the cable to force an intermittent failure. Expected outcome: The connected-version cable is either confirmed intact or identified as visibly damaged or unreliable.

steps.title: Replace Only an Approved Externally Replaceable Cable When Appropriate
steps.instructions: If the thermometer housing and sensing area are intact, but the connected-version interface cable is visibly damaged or fails normal communication testing, replace the detachable cable only with the correct approved replacement part according to facility procedure and manufacturer guidance. For the generic TAT-5000S-RS232 model, Exergen identifies a QR replacement cable as an externally replaceable component. Expected outcome: A correctly installed replacement cable seats securely, has no visible damage, and restores normal connected operation. Stop if resolved: If cable replacement restores reliable communication and the thermometer passes required functional verification, document the repair and return the unit to service per department policy.

steps.title: Verify Standalone Thermometer Function
steps.instructions: With the device removed from patient use, perform an operational check: Confirm the thermometer powers on normally; Confirm the display remains stable; Confirm no persistent error message is present; Confirm the probe button and display function consistently; Inspect again for any looseness, cracking, or unsafe surface condition. Do not use patient testing as the initial verification method for a damaged or recently repaired device. Expected outcome: The thermometer operates consistently without visible damage or errors.

steps.title: Verify Connected Operation When Applicable
steps.instructions: For RS-232/QR connected versions, reconnect the device to its intended host equipment or approved test configuration. Confirm: The cable is fully seated; Temperature data transmits appropriately; No intermittent loss of connection occurs during normal positioning; The host system receives the expected temperature result. Expected outcome: Reliable communication is restored without manipulation of the cable or connector.

steps.title: Consider Accuracy Verification Following Physical Damage
steps.instructions: If the thermometer sustained an impact, probe damage concern, or any physical event that could affect sensing accuracy, perform appropriate accuracy verification according to department policy using approved verification equipment, or send the device for manufacturer evaluation. Exergen indicates that recalibration is normally not required unless the thermometer is physically damaged or experiences component failure; verification may be performed using Exergen calibration verification equipment, while recalibration requires return to Exergen. Expected outcome: The unit is returned to service only when physical condition, operation, communication where applicable, and required accuracy verification are acceptable.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported the Exergen TAT-5000S connected thermometer had a cracked interface cable jacket near the thermometer connector and intermittently failed to transmit temperatures.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found the thermometer housing and probe intact, but the RS-232/QR interface cable had damaged strain relief and intermittent communication during normal positioning.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the thermometer from clinical use, replaced the approved external interface cable, verified stable display and reliable temperature transmission, and returned the unit to service after passing functional checks.

helpfulDetails: Location and description of physical damage
Probe head and sensing lens condition
Housing crack, separation, or sharp-edge findings
Battery cover condition
Display behavior and any error messages
Connected-version cable type and damage location
Interface communication test result
Cable replaced and replacement part information
Accuracy verification completed or repair referral made
Final device status
